item.title: 'No consent was given' - Parent speaks out over viral school preaching video
item.description: A parent whose child was present during the school session shown in a viral social media video says families were not informed or asked for consent before a preacher addressed students, and is now seeking answers from the school's administration. The parent, who requested anonymity, told fijivillage News that they only became aware of the incident after the video began circulating on Facebook yest
item.content:encoded:
A parent whose child was present during the school session shown in a viral social media video says families were not informed or asked for consent before a preacher addressed students, and is now seeking answers from the school's administration.

The parent, who requested anonymity, told fijivillage News that they only became aware of the incident after the video began circulating on Facebook yesterday.

The video, which has attracted widespread attention online , appears to show Christian preacher and content creator Osaslugo addressing students at a prominent school in Lautoka before singling out a student he identifies as Muslim.

He appears to tell the student that because he does not follow Jesus Christ or Christianity, he will not go to heaven.

Many are questioning whether it was appropriate for a student to be publicly singled out because of his religion in a school environment.

The parent adds that they are still not happy after seeing the video and are trying to arrange a meeting with the school principal to establish how the session was allowed to take place.

The parent further states that neither they nor, to their knowledge, other parents had been notified in advance that a preacher would be addressing students.

The incident has raised broader questions about whether appropriate approval processes were followed, whether parents should have been informed beforehand, and how religious activities are managed in schools.

We are still awaiting a response from the Ministry of Education regarding the incident, including whether permission was granted for the session, what safeguards were in place, and what action, if any, will be taken.

We have also sent questions to the Fiji Human Rights and Anti-Discrimination Commission.

Fiji is a secular state, and the Constitution guarantees freedom of religion and protection from discrimination on religious grounds.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: Insufficient evidence led to charges being dropped against men accused over Laqere checkpoint incident
item.description: The Office of the Director of Public Prosecutions says charges against Domoniko Tabao and Jone Rakatia over the alleged attempted murder of a female police officer at the Laqere checkpoint on the 26th of April this year were discontinued due to insufficient evidence. The ODPP confirms to fijivillage News that after careful consideration of the evidence, legal analysis and the law, the charges agai
item.content:encoded:
The Office of the Director of Public Prosecutions says charges against Domoniko Tabao and Jone Rakatia over the alleged attempted murder of a female police officer at the Laqere checkpoint on the 26th of April this year were discontinued due to insufficient evidence.

The ODPP confirms to fijivillage News that after careful consideration of the evidence, legal analysis and the law, the charges against the two men were discontinued by way of nolle prosequi because there was insufficient evidence to proceed.

The ODPP says this does not prevent the State from re-laying charges against the two men should new evidence emerge.

Meanwhile, the charges against co-accused Saimoni Rokotunidau will continue.

Rokotunidau is charged with one count of attempted murder, one count of unlawful possession of illicit drugs, one count of going equipped, one count of serious assault, and one count of refusing to have his fingerprints and photographs taken.

item.title: Jason Auto & More calls for probe into alleged damage to imported vehicles
item.description: Five imported vehicles belonging to Jason Auto & More Pte Ltd have been found with what the company says appears to be deliberate scratches and vandalism, prompting the company to call for a full police investigation into the incident. Managing Director Jason Chandra says four vehicles from their latest shipment were initially found to have sustained what appears to be deliberate scratches and van
item.content:encoded:
Five imported vehicles belonging to Jason Auto & More Pte Ltd have been found with what the company says appears to be deliberate scratches and vandalism, prompting the company to call for a full police investigation into the incident.

Managing Director Jason Chandra says four vehicles from their latest shipment were initially found to have sustained what appears to be deliberate scratches and vandalism upon collection from Suva Wharf.

Chandra says the damage was discovered during the clearance process before the vehicles were transported to the dealership.

He says he was advised by the customs clearing agent this afternoon that another vehicle had also sustained scratches, bringing the total number of affected vehicles to five.

Chandra says each vehicle had undergone an independent inspection by the Japan Export Vehicle Inspection Centre (JEVIC) before being shipped to Fiji , adding that accompanying inspection reports and photographs clearly show that the vehicles were free from the damage now observed.

He says they have formally reported the matter to the Police and requested a full criminal investigation.

He says they have also written to Fiji Ports Corporation Limited and Carpenters Shipping, requesting their urgent cooperation, including the preservation of CCTV footage, operational records and any other evidence that may assist investigators.

Chandra says, as a newly established Fijian business, the company has invested significantly in importing quality vehicles and creating employment for local people, and incidents of this nature not only result in financial loss but also undermine confidence in the security of imported cargo.

He stresses that their intention is not to assign blame to any organisation or individual; they simply seek a fair, transparent and thorough investigation to establish how the damage occurred and to ensure those responsible are held accountable.

He adds that they remain committed to supplying quality vehicles and maintaining the trust placed in the company by its customers, and are confident the relevant authorities and stakeholders will cooperate fully to determine the facts.

Police have confirmed that a report was lodged yesterday, adding that investigations are underway, and efforts are continuing to identify the person responsible.

item.title: Ju Ben excited to perform new music at CFL's 41st anniversary celebration this Saturday
item.description: Local rapper Peni Roqara, famously known as Ju Ben, is set to debut two new tracks when he takes to the stage at the CFL Gratitude Concert this Saturday, as Communications Fiji Limited celebrates its 41st anniversary. Speaking to fijivillage News, Ju Ben says he is looking forward to sharing his new music with fans and meeting the people who have supported his journey over the years.
item.content:encoded:
Local rapper Peni Roqara, famously known as Ju Ben, is set to debut two new tracks when he takes to the stage at the CFL Gratitude Concert this Saturday, as Communications Fiji Limited celebrates its 41st anniversary.

Speaking to fijivillage News, Ju Ben says he is looking forward to sharing his new music with fans and meeting the people who have supported his journey over the years.

The rapper says he is especially excited to perform for children, many of whom regularly sing along to his songs during live performances.

Ju Ben says he feels privileged to create positive music for the younger generation and hopes to use his platform to educate and motivate them.

He says his fans are the reason he continues to make music, adding that he creates songs for listeners who can relate to his experiences.

He also reflected on the role Communications Fiji Limited has played in his music career, saying the company was one of the first major broadcasters to support his music during his early days.

Insert: Ju Ben - Looking forward to the concert July 22

He is encouraging families to attend the free event at Albert Park in Suva, where he plans to perform several fan favourites, including his latest releases 'V ainaki' and ' Now You Know'

The CFL Gratitude Concert begins at 1pm on Saturday at Albert Park, with live entertainment, food stalls and family activities throughout the afternoon as the company celebrates 41 years of serving Fijians.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: Major jetty upgrade projects progressing across Fiji - Ro Filipe
item.description: Major jetty renewal and upgrade projects are progressing across Fiji as the Government moves to strengthen maritime infrastructure and support the nation's socio-economic development. Minister for Public Works, Meteorological Services and Transport, Ro Filipe Tuisawau says renewal works are continuing at Korolevu Jetty in Rewa and Vunikura Jetty in Cakaudrove Province under a China Grant, while $1
item.content:encoded:
Major jetty renewal and upgrade projects are progressing across Fiji as the Government moves to strengthen maritime infrastructure and support the nation's socio-economic development.

Minister for Public Works, Meteorological Services and Transport, Ro Filipe Tuisawau says renewal works are continuing at Korolevu Jetty in Rewa and Vunikura Jetty in Cakaudrove Province under a China Grant, while $1.8 million has been allocated for repairs and the installation of fenders at Nabouwalu Jetty.

Ro Filipe says detailed designs for the Natovi, Nabouwalu and Savusavu jetties have been completed, with funding for construction to be negotiated further with the Ministry of Finance.

He says the upgrades will include major improvements to the three berths and passenger facilities.

He adds that feasibility studies and designs for 22 maritime jetties are expected to be completed by the end of 2026 before financing negotiations and the tender process begin.

The Minister says these projects are part of the Government's wider infrastructure programme announced under the Ministry's 2026-2027 National Budget, approved by Parliament last week.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: New solar power system enhances healthcare services at Gasele Nursing Station
item.description: Residents of Gasele and nearby communities in Kadavu will now have access to more reliable healthcare services following the installation of a solar power system at the Gasele Nursing Station. The Ministry of Health and Medical Services says the new solar energy system, installed by the Core Energy team and completed earlier this month, provides the nursing station with a dependable electricity su
item.content:encoded:
Residents of Gasele and nearby communities in Kadavu will now have access to more reliable healthcare services following the installation of a solar power system at the Gasele Nursing Station.

The Ministry of Health and Medical Services says the new solar energy system, installed by the Core Energy team and completed earlier this month, provides the nursing station with a dependable electricity supply, strengthening its ability to safely store vaccines and enabling healthcare workers to deliver services more efficiently.

They say the project marks another step in its efforts to improve access to reliable and equitable healthcare for people living in Fiji's rural and maritime communities.

The Ministry has acknowledged the contributions of Core Energy, its development partners and the people of Gasele for working together to successfully complete the project, highlighting the importance of partnerships in strengthening healthcare services across the country.

Send your NEWS TIPS and VIDEOS to us on our Facebook or Instagram OR you can email us on tips@fijivillage.com.fj

item.title: Court told Kamikamica's affidavit did not add up with other evidence
item.description: The first FICAC witness in the case of former Deputy Prime Minister Manoa Kamikamica testified in court today that after the Commi
